item.title: Millom School
item.description: Millom School, formerly Millom County Secondary School, is a coeducational secondary school and sixth form located in Millom in the English county of Cumbria.[3]

item.title: Oxalis polyphylla
item.description: Oxalis polyphylla, the finger sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae.[2][3] The flower color is variable from lilac to deep pink or magenta. It is native to the southern Cape Provinces of South Africa.[1] A geophyte with a bulb that is often gummy, it can reach 20 cm (8 in) in height, and is found growing both on rocky outcrops and in flat areas.[2]

item.title: Braly Municipal Stadium
item.description: Tom Braly Municipal Stadium is a 14,215-seat stadium in Florence, Alabama. It is primarily used for American football, and is the home field of the Florence High School Falcons.[4] From 1949 to 2025, it was the home of the North Alabama Lions. It also hosted the NCAA Division II Football Championship games from 1986 to 2013, which were broadcast on ESPN.

item.title: Melanotaenium
item.description: The Melanotaenium is a genus of smut fungi in the family Melanotaeniaceae.[2][3]

item.title: 2007–08 Croatian Football Cup
item.description: The 2007–08 Croatian Football Cup was the seventeenth season of Croatia's football knockout competition. Dinamo Zagreb were the defending champion and they won it for a second year running after beating Hajduk Split 3–0 on aggregate.
